query: management of infertility with bilateral tubal blockage

retrieved_docs.title: Female Infertility -- Treatment / Management -- Tubal and Pelvic Adhesions
retrieved_docs.content: In vitro fertilization (IVF) is the first-line treatment for bilateral tubal factor infertility. Tubal corrective surgeries have worse pregnancy outcomes and have an increased risk of ectopic pregnancy. Women with severe tubal disease, including hydrosalpinx, are encouraged to have a bilateral salpingectomy to increase the pregnancy rate of IVF. [62] For women with mild distal tubal disease, fimbrioplasty is an option to allow for multiple pregnancies without IVF. One small study showed that the pregnancy rate was equal to IVF for mild tubal disease, but the risk of ectopic pregnancy was 15% compared to 0.7% for IVF treatment. [63]

retrieved_docs.title: Female Infertility -- Treatment / Management -- Tubal and Pelvic Adhesions
retrieved_docs.content: The patients with a prior bilateral salpingectomy or tubal ligation for contraception are an important tubal factor population. It is always important for healthcare providers to discuss the risk of regret with all women who desire tubal ligation. The chance of pregnancy after tubal reanastomosis depends on the patient's age, the type of ligation, and the tubal length available. Younger women who had a ring or a clip with more than 4 cm of tubal length are the best candidates and have comparable pregnancy rates to IVF. [64] However, the time to pregnancy is significantly longer following tubal surgery as compared to IVF.

retrieved_docs.title: Female Infertility -- Treatment / Management -- Controlled Ovarian Hyperstimulation
retrieved_docs.content: Higher rate of monofollicular development and a corresponding decrease in twin gestations Shorter half-life No antiestrogenic effects on the endometrium and central nervous system Lower estradiol levels, which is a benefit for women with breast cancer undergoing IVF Letrozole's beneficial profile compared to clomiphene may replace clomiphene as a first-line treatment. [58]

retrieved_docs.title: Tubal Sterilization -- Preparation -- Obtaining Informed Consent
retrieved_docs.content: Nature of the procedure : The planned tubal sterilization technique should be discussed with the patient. However, discussing alternative approaches with the patient is also prudent if intraoperative factors (eg, adhesive disease) make one method more complex or riskier. Additionally, the same technique does not have to be applied to both tubes for the procedure to be effective. For instance, if a bilateral salpingectomy is planned, but significant tubal adhesions are encountered unexpectedly on the right side, the right side may be occluded with a clip while the left tube is completely excised. The potential for scenarios like this should be discussed with the patient beforehand.

retrieved_docs.title: Tubal Sterilization -- Technique or Treatment -- Bilateral salpingectomy
retrieved_docs.content: The tube is excised using laparoscopic instruments, typically electrosurgical devices with coagulation and cutting abilities. To completely excise the tube, all of the attachment points must be transected, including the proximal isthmus immediately adjacent to the uterine cornua, the mesosalpinx immediately underneath the entire length of the tube out to the fimbriated end, and the tuboovarian ligament. The procedure can be accomplished by working from the medial side to the lateral side or vice versa. Following removal from the abdominal cavity, the tubal specimens should be sent for pathologic evaluation. Finally, hemostasis should be assured before removing all surgical instruments and closing the laparoscopic port sites. [33] [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Sonography Gynecology Infertility Assessment, Protocols, and Interpretation -- Clinical Significance -- Importance of tubal Assessment
retrieved_docs.content: The presence of hydrosalpinges reduced live birth rates by 30% due to the reflux of toxic fluid into the endometrium. [49] [50] Ultrasound findings of hydrosalpinx include thick or thin-walled, tubular, or contorted S-shaped anechoic fluid-filled structure with partial septa.  (Figure 19) Sometimes the hydrosalpinx looks like “beads on a string.” ASRM recommends laparoscopic salpingectomy or proximal tubal occlusion with cautery or partial salpingectomy to improve subsequent pregnancy and live birth rates through IVF  in patients with hydrosalpinges. [51]

retrieved_docs.title: Female Infertility -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: The World Health Organization (WHO) performed a large multinational study to determine gender distribution and infertility etiologies. In 37% of infertile couples, female infertility was the cause; in 35% of couples, both male and female causes were identified; in 8%, there was male factor infertility. [3] In the same study, the most common identifiable factors of female infertility are as follows: Ovulatory disorders: 25% Endometriosis: 15% Pelvic adhesions: 12% Tubal blockage: 11% Other tubal/uterine abnormalities: 11% Hyperprolactinemia: 7%

retrieved_docs.title: Tubal Sterilization -- Technique or Treatment -- Bilateral salpingectomy
retrieved_docs.content: The patient is placed in a dorsal lithotomy position under general anesthesia and surgically prepared and draped using standard aseptic techniques. The patient's bladder should then be emptied with a urinary catheter. The surgeon usually will conduct a pelvic exam and place a uterine manipulator, carefully changing gloves before beginning the abdominal portion of the procedure to prevent cross-contamination. [31] Standard laparoscopic techniques are used to enter and insufflate the abdomen, which commonly only requires 1 or 2 port sites. [32] The abdomen and pelvic cavities should be inspected, and the fallopian tubes should be followed out to their fimbriated ends to confirm the proper structure has been identified, as the proximal fallopian tube may be easily confused with the round ligament. If adhesions or abnormal anatomy are present, the ureters should be identified to avoid inadvertent ureteral injury. Using standard surgical techniques, pelvic adhesions limiting tubal mobility can be carefully taken down.

retrieved_docs.title: Female Infertility -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: The 3 primary complications associated with infertility treatments are multiples, ectopic pregnancy, and ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome.

retrieved_docs.title: Tubal Ligation (Archived) -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Post-tubal ligation syndrome is a potential complication that is well-documented in the lay media. Its actual existence, however, controversial. Symptoms may include dyspareunia, low back pain, premenstrual tension syndrome, menstrual abnormalities (missed periods, heavy menstrual bleeding), or menopausal symptoms. [13] The etiology of post-tubal ligation syndrome may be related to adnexal vascular disruption. [13] Treatment may be symptomatic if patient bother is mild. However, some patients may require/request surgical management, such as reversal of the tubal ligation. [13]

retrieved_docs.title: Female Infertility -- Treatment / Management -- Uterine Abnormalities
retrieved_docs.content: There is unclear evidence on the effect of leiomyomas on infertility and live birth rates. It is recommended that the patient receive a complete infertility workup before further investigation into fibroids. The most important aspect of fibroids is the location. Fibroids that impinge on the endometrium and distort the uterine cavity result in impaired implantation and increased miscarriage rates. [65] Women with submucosal or submucosal-intramural fibroids that distort the uterine cavity have been proven to have decreased pregnancy rates. With the removal of these fibroids, pregnancy, and live birth rates increase. [66] The first-line treatment for the removal of the most detrimental fibroids is operative hysteroscopy. Other uterine pathologies like uterine synechiae and septa are more related to recurrent pregnancy loss but are capable of causing infertility. Operative hysteroscopy has shown a marked reduction in pregnancy loss for women with both synechiae and septa. [67] Asymptomatic polyps have also been shown to cause infertility. One study showed a polypectomy on asymptomatic infertile women before IUI increased pregnancy rates from 28% to 63%. [68]

retrieved_docs.title: Salpingitis Isthmica Nodosa -- Treatment / Management -- Surgery
retrieved_docs.content: Reconstructive proximal surgery, originally described in the 1890s, was the standard treatment for decades and has been shown to yield relatively high pregnancy rates of 34%. [23] It has gradually been superseded with the introduction of microsurgical procedures, which carry fewer surgical risks and have been shown to lead to pregnancy rates as high as 68% within the first 2 years following the procedure. [24] These procedures appear to deliver the best outcomes in females under 37 who undergo bilateral anastomosis. Proximal tubal patency can also be restored using transcervical catheterization, an even less invasive procedure. Although this is associated with lower pregnancy rates, transcervical catheterization is occasionally preferred due to its decreased morbidity, reduced cost, and ability to maintain tubular length. [25] Finally, a salpingectomy is recommended for symptomatic patients where fertility is not an issue. [26]

retrieved_docs.title: Sonography Gynecology Infertility Assessment, Protocols, and Interpretation -- Clinical Significance -- Importance of tubal Assessment
retrieved_docs.content: Evaluation of tubal patency plays a key role in counseling infertility patients considering IUI or IVF therapies.  Sonohysterosalpingogram (HycoSY) is a quick, simple technique to confirm tubal patency rapidly. A combined SIS with 2D or 3D ultrasound can be performed around day 5 through 9 as a “one-stop-shop” infertility evaluation. [52] HyCoSy is comparable to HSG for assessment of tubal patency when compared with the laparoscopic chromopertubation. [53]

retrieved_docs.title: Tubal Sterilization -- Technique or Treatment -- Bilateral salpingectomy
retrieved_docs.content: Laparoscopic bilateral salpingectomy is becoming the preferred method of tubal sterilization because the procedure is associated with lower rates of ovarian cancer without increasing surgical risk. Though long-term data is not yet available, bilateral salpingectomy is theoretically the most effective sterilization procedure. Additionally, salpingectomy is often preferred if the tube appears grossly abnormal (eg, hydrosalpinx). [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Young Syndrome -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Surgical interventions, including microsurgical reconstruction of the vas and/or epididymis or transurethral resection of the ejaculatory ducts (TURED), might be utilized. However, similar to all couples with the complaint of infertility, before performing the microsurgery in the male partner, diagnostic protocols to evaluate the female partner should not be underestimated. Fertility procedural treatment options might be well categorized into a surgical intervention to ablate the obstruction in the epididymis; A) Vasoepididymostomy or B) Assisted reproduction procedures , including intracytoplasmic sperm injection (ICSI). Generally, the microsurgical interventions, including vasoepididymostomy to address the obstruction, are more cost-effective than IVF/ICSI. However, sperm retrieval with ICSI might be preferred over vasoepididymostomy in patients with a long-term obstructive issue. [19] Moreover, airway and sinus complications, including bronchiectasis and sinusitis, might be treated symptomatically.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 1779
retrieved_docs.content: استئصال قناة فالوب (salpingectomy) is categorized under Female Reproductive System Procedures and functions as a parent entity for several specific laparoscopic techniques. The procedures Laparoscopic partial salpingectomy, bilateral; Laparoscopic partial salpingectomy, unilateral; Laparoscopic salpingectomy, bilateral; and Laparoscopic salpingectomy, unilateral are all child procedures of استئصال قناة فالوب, representing bilateral or unilateral removal of the fallopian tube, either partially or completely, using minimally invasive laparoscopy. Clinically, استئصال قناة فالوب frequently co‑occurs with مرض التهاب الحوض (pelvic inflammatory disease), reflecting its common use in the management of this infection and its complications. Recognizing the hierarchical relationship of these procedures within female reproductive system interventions and their association with مرض التهاب الحوض helps clinicians select appropriate surgical approaches and anticipate related clinical scenarios.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 4 -- Community 2788
retrieved_docs.content: Salpingectomy (استئصال قناة فالوب) is a surgical procedure that involves the removal of one or both fallopian tubes and is classified within the broader category of Female Reproductive System Procedures. Two common minimally invasive variants are the unilateral laparoscopic partial salpingectomy and the bilateral laparoscopic salpingectomy, both of which are specific subtypes of salpingectomy. Clinically, salpingectomy is associated with an increased risk of abnormal pregnancy (حمل منتبذ), reflecting its impact on tubal function and fertility. The procedure also frequently co‑occurs with pelvic inflammatory disease (مرض التهاب الحوض), indicating that patients with PID may require salpingectomy as part of their management. Understanding these relationships helps clinicians anticipate reproductive outcomes, recognize associated infections, and select the appropriate surgical approach for optimal patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2166
retrieved_docs.content: Intrauterine pregnancy is clinically linked to several obstetric interventions and outcomes. It often leads to the use of labor induction (تحفيز المخاض) to initiate delivery when medically indicated, and in some cases delivery may be performed with forceps (الولادة بالملقط) to assist the birth process. Although intrauterine pregnancy itself signifies successful conception, certain underlying conditions or complications associated with the pregnancy can also be related to subsequent infertility (العقم), highlighting the need for careful evaluation of reproductive health before and after pregnancy. Understanding these interconnections helps clinicians anticipate appropriate labor management strategies and address potential fertility concerns in patient care.

Corresponding left‑sided procedures are radical trachelectomy with left ovary removal and left fallopian tube‑ovary resection (استئصال البوق والمبيض في جهة اليسار). The module “OVARIES AND FALLOPIAN TUBES: EXCISIONS”, a child of both oophorectomy and other operations on fallopian tubes, contains partial salpingo‑oophorectomy and partial salpingo‑oophorotomy, which co‑occur with partial hysterectomy and are children of the broader “OVARIES AND FALLOPIAN TUBES: GENERAL AND MISCELLANEOUS OPERATIVE PROCEDURES” category. Management of ectopic pregnancy is organized under “removal of ectopic pregnancy” and includes fetotoxic injection via laparoscopy, laparoscopic salpingotomy with tubal pregnancy removal, and laparoscopic salpingectomy with tubal pregnancy removal—the principal surgical approaches for tubal ectopic gestations. Gynecologic hysterectomy procedures are linked to the female reproductive

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 1 -- Community 205
retrieved_docs.content: of menstruation”) are localized to these organs, with infertility positioned within female reproductive function. The infectious process is identified as the underlying mechanism of post‑abortion complications, linking infection directly to adverse outcomes after termination procedures. Collectively, this structured framework interrelates prenatal surveillance, invasive diagnostics, therapeutic interventions, delivery assistance, placental and ectopic‑pregnancy management, and post‑delivery care, providing a comprehensive reference for clinical documentation, education, and patient‑centered obstetric management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 3 -- Community 2423
retrieved_docs.content: Intrauterine pregnancy, the condition of a developing embryo within the uterine cavity, is clinically associated with several reproductive health issues. It has been linked to infertility (العقم), indicating that women who experience an intrauterine pregnancy may also encounter challenges conceiving in subsequent cycles or that underlying factors contributing to infertility can coexist with a confirmed gestation. Intrauterine pregnancy is also associated with endometritis (الْتِهابُ بِطانَةِ الرَّحِمِ), an inflammation of the uterine lining that can arise during or after pregnancy and may affect maternal outcomes. Additionally, intrauterine pregnancy is clinically associated with tubal ligation (ربط البوق), a permanent contraceptive procedure that may be considered after delivery or in the context of managing reproductive health following a pregnancy. Recognizing these associations helps clinicians monitor for potential complications, counsel patients about future fertility, and plan appropriate postpartum contraceptive strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 2025
retrieved_docs.content: Endoscopy of ovary and fallopian tube, ligation and crushing of bilateral fallopian tubes, and ligation of bilateral fallopian tubes are interrelated gynecologic procedures that fall under the overarching category of ligation or occlusion of fallopian tubes. Within this hierarchy, ligation and crushing of bilateral fallopian tubes is identified as a specific form of both ligation of bilateral fallopian tubes and the broader ligation or occlusion of fallopian tubes, indicating that it combines tubal ligation with mechanical crushing. Similarly, Endoscopy of ovary and fallopian tube is classified as a subprocedure of ligation or occlusion of fallopian tubes, reflecting its role in the diagnostic or therapeutic evaluation of the tubal structures. Recognizing these hierarchical relationships helps clinicians understand the spectrum of interventions ranging from diagnostic endoscopy to definitive tubal occlusion techniques, facilitating appropriate procedural selection and patient counseling.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 3 -- Community 2155
retrieved_docs.content: The procedure ربط البوق is classified as ligation or occlusion of the fallopian tubes, representing a surgical method of permanent female sterilization. Clinical observations indicate that حَمْلٌ بُوقِيّ, a tubal (ectopic) pregnancy, can co‑occur with ربط البوق, suggesting an association between tubal ligation and the occurrence of tubal pregnancies. Recognizing this relationship is important for patient counseling, risk assessment, and postoperative monitoring.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 2 -- Community 1045
retrieved_docs.content: with laparoscopic salpingectomy, as is pelvic inflammatory disease (مرض التهاب الحوض). These co‑occurrences reflect the overlap of tubal surgery with ovarian cyst management and treatment of pelvic inflammatory disease. Overall, laparoscopic salpingectomy—whether partial, unilateral, or bilateral—serves as a central, minimally invasive strategy for treating ectopic (tubal) pregnancy, ovarian cysts, and pelvic inflammatory disease, and it provides a foundational element for more extensive procedures such as salpingo‑oophorectomy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 2 -- Community 1089
retrieved_docs.content: Salpingectomy (استئصال قناة فالوب) is a female reproductive system procedure classified under operations on the fallopian tubes (العمليات الجراحية لقناة فالوب) and abdominal surgery (العمليات الجراحية في البطن). It involves removal of one or both fallopian tubes (vejcovody, فَغْرُ البُوْق) and serves as a definitive treatment for tubal (ectopic) pregnancy (حمل منتبذ), an inverse relationship indicating that removal of an ectopic pregnancy often results in salpingectomy. Salpingectomy frequently co‑occurs with pelvic inflammatory disease (مرض التهاب الحوض) and tubal‑ovarian abscess (خراج بوقي مبيضي), reflecting its role in managing complications of these pelvic infections. Laparoscopy (GI only), a minimally invasive abdominal surgical approach, encompasses a range of child procedures that target the fallopian tubes, including bilateral and unilateral laparoscopic partial salpingectomy, bilateral and unilateral laparoscopic salpingectomy, laparoscopic salpingostomy, laparoscopic

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 4 -- Community 2840
retrieved_docs.content: استئصال قناة فالوب (salpingectomy) is a surgical procedure that involves removal of one or both fallopian tubes and is clinically associated with حمل منتبذ (tubal/ectopic pregnancy), reflecting its role in managing or preventing this condition. Several minimally invasive variants of this procedure are recognized, including Laparoscopic partial salpingectomy, bilateral; Laparoscopic salpingectomy, bilateral; and Laparoscopic salpingectomy, unilateral. Each of these laparoscopic techniques is considered a specific child procedure of the general استئصال قناة فالوب, illustrating the hierarchical classification of surgical approaches. Moreover, استئصال قناة فالوب is categorized under Female Reproductive System Procedures, indicating that it is a specialized member of the broader group of interventions targeting the female reproductive tract. Understanding these interconnections helps clinicians select appropriate surgical methods, recognize the clinical contexts such as ectopic pregnancy in which salpingectomy is indicated, and place the procedure within the overall framework of reproductive health care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 2535
retrieved_docs.content: Female infertility (العقم لدى الإناث) and endometrial implantation (انتباذ بطاني رحمي) are interconnected medical conditions. Prevention and control measures are applicable to both female infertility and endometrial implantation, and the two conditions are related, indicating that endometrial implantation can contribute to or be associated with female infertility and that managing endometrial pathology is important in the care of women with infertility.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 3 -- Community 2365
retrieved_docs.content: Ligation and crushing of bilateral fallopian tubes is a specific surgical technique that falls under the broader category of ligation of bilateral fallopian tubes and, more generally, under the umbrella of ligation or occlusion of fallopian tubes. This procedure combines both tying and crushing of the bilateral fallopian tubes to achieve permanent occlusion, representing a particular method within the range of tubal ligation approaches used for permanent contraception.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 2099
retrieved_docs.content: Intrauterine pregnancy is clinically associated with both Ligation of bilateral fallopian tubes and الْتِهابُ بِطانَةِ الرَّحِمِ (endometritis). The association with Ligation of bilateral fallopian tubes reflects the possibility of tubal sterilization failure, whereby a pregnancy can occur despite bilateral tubal occlusion. The link with الْتِهابُ بِطانَةِ الرَّحِمِ underscores that intrauterine pregnancy may be complicated by inflammation of the uterine lining, which can affect maternal and fetal outcomes. Ligation and crushing of bilateral fallopian tubes is a specific form of Ligation of bilateral fallopian tubes, representing a more detailed surgical technique used to achieve tubal occlusion. Understanding these interconnections is important for clinicians managing contraceptive counseling, recognizing potential pregnancy after sterilization, and addressing infectious complications such as endometritis in pregnant patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1897
retrieved_docs.content: Endoscopy of ovary and fallopian tube is classified as a subprocedure within the broader category of ligation or occlusion of fallopian tubes, indicating that it is related to interventions involving the fallopian tubes; while endoscopy provides a visual diagnostic examination of the ovary and fallopian tube, ligation or occlusion of fallopian tubes refers to surgical techniques used to block or seal the tubes for sterilization purposes.
